title: He Awa Whiria: Braided River - Understanding Ngāti Kahungunu Cultural Landscapes
subject: Social Studies
culturalContext: Te Awa o Nūhaka - The sacred river of Ngāti Kahungunu that flows through our rohe, connecting our people to their whenua, whakapapa, and traditional practices of resource management and environmental guardianship
learningObjectives: Analyze how Te Awa o Nūhaka embodies Ngāti Kahungunu values of kaitiakitanga and whanaungatanga
Investigate historical and contemporary relationships between the local community and the river ecosystem
Create evidence-based recommendations for sustainable river management that honor both mātauranga Māori and scientific approaches
Demonstrate understanding of cultural landscapes through mapping and storytelling techniques

activities.title: Whakapapa o Te Awa - River Genealogy
activities.description: Students explore the whakapapa of Te Awa o Nūhaka through local pūrākau (stories) and create visual timelines showing the river's significance to Ngāti Kahungunu across generations

activities.title: Waiography - River Mapping
activities.description: Using GIS technology and traditional Māori mapping techniques, students create layered maps showing cultural sites, traditional fishing grounds, and current environmental monitoring data along the river

activities.title: Kōrero with Kaitiaki
activities.description: Students interview local kaumātua and environmental kaitiaki about their relationship with the river, documenting traditional knowledge and contemporary conservation practices

activities.title: Te Awa Health Assessment
activities.description: Conduct field studies using both scientific water testing methods and cultural indicators (presence of native species, water clarity) to assess the current health of the river

activities.title: Future Guardians Project
activities.description: Students develop and present a kaitiakitanga action plan for Te Awa o Nūhaka, integrating mātauranga Māori with modern conservation science

resources: Ngāti Kahungunu Iwi Incorporated environmental reports and cultural archives
Local kaumātua and kaitiaki from Nūhaka community
NIWA River Ecosystems monitoring toolkit and cultural health assessment framework
Te Ara Encyclopedia of New Zealand - Ngāti Kahungunu stories and history

tasks: Produce a detailed cultural landscape report of Te Awa o Nūhaka
Create a multimedia presentation proposing specific kaitiakitanga actions
Develop a comparative analysis of traditional and scientific river management approaches

nzcAlignment: Social Studies Level 5: Understand how cultural practices vary but reflect similar purposes
Social Studies Level 5: Understand how people's management of resources impacts on environmental and social sustainability
Social Studies Level 5: Understand how the ideas and actions of people in the past have had a significant impact on people's lives
Social Studies Level 5: Understand how people define and seek human rights
